page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores using an Intel Xeon 8163 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, and the instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-threaded execution. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%. However, the 64 physical cores deliver competitive parallel processing capabilities. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU extended instructions tests both rank in the top 25% tier. General multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and stress-ng multi-core, consistently register in the average middle 50% tier.

The hardware profile and benchmark results suggest a clear tradeoff. While single-threaded tasks are bottlenecked by the lower per-core performance of the Intel Xeon 8163 processor, highly parallelized workloads scale effectively across the 64 dedicated cores. This makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded applications such as software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving, which all achieved average tier results. It is also capable of handling moderate database operations and medium-sized LLM inference tasks. However, it is not recommended for workloads dependent on high single-core throughput or local high-speed storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs clocked at 2.5 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the lower tiers, whereas multi-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression and Passmark extended instructions perform in the top 25%. General multi-core benchmarks, database operations, and static web serving consistently achieve average tier results. This instance is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, including multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and moderate database operations, where tasks can be distributed across its 64 physical cores.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-threaded performance.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core compute-optimized virtual server designed for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads and moderate database operations.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage.
Strong multi-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression and extended instructions.
Weak single-core performance, making it less suitable for single-threaded tasks.
Well-suited for parallel software compilation, ray tracing, and web serving.
